smoothness.label: Smoothness
smoothness.description: All disjuncts mention the same variables

read-once.label: Read-Once
read-once.description: Each variable appears at most once on any path

CO.label: Consistency
CO.description: Check if the formula is satisfiable

VA.label: Validity
VA.description: Check if the formula is a tautology

CE.label: Clausal Entailment
CE.description: Check if a clause is entailed

IM.label: Implicant
IM.description: Find an implicant of the formula

EQ.label: Equivalence
EQ.description: Check logical equivalence of two formulas

SE.label: Sentential Entailment
SE.description: Check if one formula entails another

CT.label: Model Counting
CT.description: Count the number of satisfying assignments

ME.label: Model Enumeration
ME.description: Enumerate all satisfying assignments

CD.label: Conditioning
CD.description: Restrict formula given variable assignments

FO.label: Forgetting
FO.description: Existentially quantify out variables

SFO.label: Singleton Forgetting
SFO.description: Forget a single variable

AND_C.label: Conjunction
AND_C.description: Compute conjunction of formulas

AND_BC.label: Bounded Conjunction
AND_BC.description: Conjunction with bounded result size

OR_C.label: Disjunction
OR_C.description: Compute disjunction of formulas

OR_BC.label: Bounded Disjunction
OR_BC.description: Disjunction with bounded result size

NOT_C.label: Negation
NOT_C.description: Negate the formula

matrix.description: \edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_89649e36}{lang_3bebcab7} in quasipolynomial time. If CNF compiled to cSDD in quasipolynomial time, then CNF would compile to DNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore CNF cannot compile to cSDD in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_6c130090}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_89649e36}{lang_3bebcab7} in quasipolynomial time. If CNF compiled to d-DNNF in quasipolynomial time, then CNF would compile to DNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore CNF cannot compile to d-DNNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_ea9b5299}{lang_6c130090} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_89649e36}{lang_6c130090} in quasipolynomial time. If CNF compiled to d-SDNNF in quasipolynomial time, then CNF would compile to d-DNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore CNF cannot compile to d-SDNNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_6c130090} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_89649e36}{lang_6c130090} in quasipolynomial time. If CNF compiled to d-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then CNF would compile to d-DNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore CNF cannot compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_89649e36}{lang_3bebcab7} in quasipolynomial time. If CNF compiled to DNF in quasipolynomial time, then CNF would compile to DNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore CNF cannot compile to DNF in quasipolynomial time.

\citet[Theorem 1]{Bova_2014} gives a family of \langref{CNF} formulas whose every \langref{DNNF} representation has exponential size, so \langref{CNF} cannot compile to \langref{DNNF} in quasipolynomial size.

\edgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_6c130090} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_89649e36}{lang_6c130090} in quasipolynomial time. If CNF compiled to FBDD in quasipolynomial time, then CNF would compile to d-DNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore CNF cannot compile to FBDD in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_6ae90adc}{lang_4c204bf3} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_89649e36}{lang_4c204bf3} in quasipolynomial time. If CNF compiled to IP in quasipolynomial time, then CNF would compile to DNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore CNF cannot compile to IP in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_e02902d0}{lang_83e3b023} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_89649e36}{lang_83e3b023} in quasipolynomial time. If CNF compiled to MODS in quasipolynomial time, then CNF would compile to cSDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore CNF cannot compile to MODS in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_1df07cc3}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_89649e36}{lang_3bebcab7} in quasipolynomial time. If CNF compiled to nFBDD in quasipolynomial time, then CNF would compile to DNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore CNF cannot compile to nFBDD in quasipolynomial time.
As defined by \citet{Darwiche_2002}, \langref{CNF} is a subset of \langref{NNF}. Therefore, a polynomial compilation is trivial.

\edgeref{lang_d24efe0e}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_89649e36}{lang_3bebcab7} in quasipolynomial time. If CNF compiled to nOBDD in quasipolynomial time, then CNF would compile to DNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore CNF cannot compile to nOBDD in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_83e3b023} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_89649e36}{lang_83e3b023} in quasipolynomial time. If CNF compiled to OBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time, then CNF would compile to cSDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore CNF cannot compile to OBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time.
It is well-known that some \langref{CNF} formulas (i.e. the negation of the parity function $\neg PARITY_n=\neg\bigoplus_{i=1}^n x_i$) have exponentially many prime implicants.

\edgeref{lang_1afefbe2}{lang_6c130090} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_89649e36}{lang_6c130090} in quasipolynomial time. If CNF compiled to SDD in quasipolynomial time, then CNF would compile to d-DNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore CNF cannot compile to SDD in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_9c84a267}{lang_6c130090} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_89649e36}{lang_6c130090} in quasipolynomial time. If CNF compiled to SDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then CNF would compile to d-DNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore CNF cannot compile to SDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_b13b0d78}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_89649e36}{lang_3bebcab7} in quasipolynomial time. If CNF compiled to SDNNF in quasipolynomial time, then CNF would compile to DNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore CNF cannot compile to SDNNF in quasipolynomial time.

\citet[Theorem 4]{Bova_2016_a} constructs a generalized hidden weighted bit function $F_n$ on $2n+1$ variables with \langref{cSDD} size $O(n^3)$ but \langref{OBDD} size $2^{\Omega(n)}$. The function $F_n$ augments $HWB_n$ with fresh variables $y_0, \ldots, y_n$ to ensure the resulting \langref{SDD} is compressed; conditioning $F_n$ on $y_i = 1$ recovers $HWB_n$, preserving Bryant's exponential \langref{OBDD} lower bound.

As formalized by \citet{Darwiche_2002} and originally introduced in \citet{Darwiche_2001a}, \langref{d-DNNF} is a subset of \langref{DNNF} satisfying the determinism property, making a polynomial compilation trivial.

\langref{d-DNNF} formulas can polynomially represent the disjunction of two functions with conflicting variable orders, which forces \langref{FBDD}{s} to grow exponentially to track the state of both orders within a single "read-once" path.

Therefore d-DNNF cannot compile to IP in quasipolynomial time.
The $OR_n$ function $\bigvee_{i=1}^nx_i$ can be represented in $O(n)$ as a \langref{d-DNNF} formula but has $2^n-1$ models.

\citet[Theorem 1]{Vinall-Smeeth_2024} proves that there are functions with structured deterministic \langref{DNNF} representations of size $s$ but whose every equivalent \langref{SDD} has size $s^{\widetilde{\Omega}(\log s)}$. The source side is genuinely structured: in the proof of Theorem 2, the hard function is an unambiguous \langref{DNF} $\psi'$, and the authors fix a vtree $T$ over $\operatorname{var}(\psi')$; each term of $\psi'$ is compiled as a \langref{d-DNNF} respecting $T$, and their disjunction still respects $T$, with determinism following from unambiguity of $\psi'$. Theorem 1 then transfers this to a \langref{SDD} lower bound using closure of \langref{SDD}s under negation.

Therefore d-SDNNF cannot compile to SDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.
As defined by \citet{Amarilli_2018}, \langref{d-SDNNF} is exactly the subset of \langref{SDNNF} satisfying determinism, so compilation is trivial.

\citet[Theorem 1 and proof of Theorem 2]{Vinall-Smeeth_2024} gives small source circuits that are explicitly structured: for the hard unambiguous \langref{DNF} $\psi'$, the proof fixes a vtree $T$ over $\operatorname{var}(\psi')$, compiles each term as a \langref{d-DNNF} respecting $T$, and takes their deterministic disjunction. Thus the source side can be stated as \langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}. The lower bound is against every equivalent \langref{SDD}; since \langfam{SDD}{T} is a subfamily of \langref{SDD}, it also rules out every target vtree family member.

For any fixed vtree $T$, \langfam{d-SDNNF}{T} is exactly the subset of \langfam{SDNNF}{T} satisfying determinism, so compilation is trivial.

Therefore d-SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to uFBDD in polynomial time.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\edgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_43a33aec} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_43a33aec}{lang_c2df8c2b} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_c2df8c2b}{lang_4e62a038} in polynomial time. Therefore d-SDNNF$_T$ compiles to uFBDD in quasipolynomial time.

quasiDescription.description: The common-order quasipolynomial simulation of \citet{TCZ_Forthcoming} specializes to \langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}: for every fixed source vtree $T$, it chooses one output order $<$ depending only on $T$; determinism guarantees that the output is a \langfam{uOBDD}{<}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\edgeref{lang_43a33aec}{lang_1df07cc3} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_1df07cc3} in polynomial time. If d-SDNNF$_T$ compiled to uOBDD$_<$ in polynomial time, then d-SDNNF$_T$ would compile to nFBDD in polynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore d-SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to uOBDD$_<$ in polynomial time.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
The common-order quasipolynomial simulation of \citet{TCZ_Forthcoming} specializes to \langfam{d-SDNNF}{T}: for every fixed source vtree $T$, it chooses one output order $<$ depending only on $T$; determinism guarantees that the output is a \langfam{uOBDD}{<}.

\edgeref{lang_8cf1da0e}{lang_9c84a267} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_9c84a267} in polynomial time. If d-SDNNF$_T$ compiled to TDD$_T$ in polynomial time, then d-SDNNF$_T$ would compile to SDD$_T$ in polynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore d-SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to TDD$_T$ in polynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_6ae90adc}{lang_4c204bf3} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_6ae90adc}{lang_89649e36} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to CNF in quasipolynomial time, then IP would compile to CNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to CNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to cSDD in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to cSDD in quasipolynomial time.

matrix.assumption: the polynomial hierarchy does not collapse
matrix.description: Because counting the models of a \langref{d-DNNF} is in P and counting the models of a \langref{DNF} is \#P-complete, \langref{DNF} cannot be polynomially compiled into \langref{d-DNNF} unconditionally. Such a compilation implies P = \#P, collapsing the polynomial hierarchy \citep{Darwiche_2002}.

\citet[Corollary 7.6]{Amarilli_2018} gives a lower bound of $2^{\Omega(tw(\phi))}$ on the smallest \langref{d-SDNNF} computing any monotone \langref{DNF} of constant arity and degree. In particular, \langref{DNF} families whose treewidth grows linearly with their size force exponential-size \langref{d-SDNNF} representations.

\edgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to d-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.
As defined by \citet{Darwiche_2002}, \langref{DNF} is a subset of \langref{DNNF}. Therefore, a polynomial compilation is trivial.

\edgeref{lang_6ae90adc}{lang_4c204bf3} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_6ae90adc}{lang_684b1ca7} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to FBDD in quasipolynomial time, then IP would compile to FBDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to FBDD in quasipolynomial time.
It is well-known that some \langref{DNF} formulas (i.e. the parity function $PARITY_n=\bigoplus_{i=1}^n x_i$) have exponentially many prime implicates.

\edgeref{lang_e02902d0}{lang_89649e36} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_89649e36} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to MODS in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to CNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to MODS in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_e827cf31} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_e827cf31}{lang_d24efe0e} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_d24efe0e}{lang_1df07cc3} in polynomial time. Therefore DNF compiles to nFBDD in polynomial time.
\edgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_3bebcab7}{lang_5bf00851} in polynomial time. Therefore DNF compiles to NNF in polynomial time.
\edgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_e827cf31} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_e827cf31}{lang_d24efe0e} in polynomial time. Therefore DNF compiles to nOBDD in polynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_83e3b023} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_83e3b023} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to OBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to cSDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to OBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_27fffab2}{lang_89649e36} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_89649e36} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to PI in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to CNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to PI in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_1afefbe2}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to SDD in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to SDD in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_9c84a267}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to SDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to SDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_e827cf31} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_e827cf31}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_3c803ba1}{lang_b13b0d78} in polynomial time. Therefore DNF compiles to SDNNF in polynomial time.
\edgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_e827cf31} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_e827cf31}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. Therefore DNF compiles to SDNNF$_T$ in polynomial time.

matrix.assumption: the polynomial hierarchy does not collapse
matrix.description: \edgeref{lang_4e62a038}{lang_6c130090} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_6c130090} in polynomial time assuming the polynomial hierarchy does not collapse. If DNF compiled to uFBDD in polynomial time, then DNF would compile to d-DNNF in polynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to uFBDD in polynomial time assuming the polynomial hierarchy does not collapse.

\edgeref{lang_c2df8c2b}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to uOBDD in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to uOBDD in quasipolynomial time.

\citet{Devadas_1993} showed that there exist Boolean functions with polynomial-size \langref{DNF} representations but requiring exponential-size \langref{OBDD} representations for any variable ordering.

\edgeref{lang_981b62f0}{lang_684b1ca7} in quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_684b1ca7} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to dec-DNNF in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to FBDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to dec-DNNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_0f27d539}{lang_83e3b023} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_83e3b023} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to dec-SDNNF in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to cSDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to dec-SDNNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_4ae03bc8}{lang_83e3b023} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_83e3b023} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to dec-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to cSDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to dec-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_82fa749e}{lang_83e3b023} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_83e3b023} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to cSDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to cSDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to cSDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

Fix any variable order $<$. Each term of a \langref{DNF} can be represented as an \langfam{OBDD}{<} of linear size (a chain of decision nodes for each literal in the term). Their nondeterministic disjunction yields an \langfam{nOBDD}{<} of size $O(|\phi|)$ because every branch follows the same fixed order.

\edgeref{lang_43a33aec}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to uOBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to uOBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_d7403a53}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to TDD in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to TDD in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_8cf1da0e}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If DNF compiled to TDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNF cannot compile to TDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_89649e36} in quasipolynomial time. If DNNF compiled to CNF in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to CNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNNF cannot compile to CNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_6c130090}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_6c130090}{lang_83e3b023} in quasipolynomial time. If DNNF compiled to cSDD in quasipolynomial time, then d-DNNF would compile to cSDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNNF cannot compile to cSDD in quasipolynomial time.

\citet[Proposition 7]{Bova_2016} shows that the Sauerhoff function $S_n$ has \langref{DNNF} size $O(n^2)$. Theorem 9 shows that $S_n$ has \langref{d-DNNF} size $2^{\Omega(n)}$. (Also see \citet{Bova_2014}.)

\edgeref{lang_6c130090}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_6c130090}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If DNNF compiled to d-SDNNF in quasipolynomial time, then d-DNNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNNF cannot compile to d-SDNNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If DNNF compiled to d-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNNF cannot compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4c204bf3} in quasipolynomial time. If DNNF compiled to DNF in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to DNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNNF cannot compile to DNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_6c130090}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_6c130090}{lang_684b1ca7} in quasipolynomial time. If DNNF compiled to FBDD in quasipolynomial time, then d-DNNF would compile to FBDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNNF cannot compile to FBDD in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_6ae90adc}{lang_4c204bf3} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4c204bf3} in quasipolynomial time. If DNNF compiled to IP in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to DNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNNF cannot compile to IP in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_e02902d0}{lang_89649e36} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_89649e36} in quasipolynomial time. If DNNF compiled to MODS in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to CNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNNF cannot compile to MODS in quasipolynomial time.

quasiDescription.description: \citet[Section 5]{Bodlaender_1993} extends the compilation of \langref{dec-DNNF}s into \langref{FBDD}s (shown in \citet{Beame_2013}) to show that \langref{DNNF} can be quasipolynomial-time compiled into \langref{nFBDD}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\edgeref{lang_6c130090}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_6c130090}{lang_1df07cc3} in polynomial time. If DNNF compiled to nFBDD in polynomial time, then d-DNNF would compile to nFBDD in polynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNNF cannot compile to nFBDD in polynomial time.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\citet[Section 5]{Bodlaender_1993} extends the compilation of \langref{dec-DNNF}s into \langref{FBDD}s (shown in \citet{Beame_2013}) to show that \langref{DNNF} can be quasipolynomial-time compiled into \langref{nFBDD}.
As defined by \citet{Darwiche_2002}, \langref{DNNF} is a subset of \langref{NNF}, so a polynomial compilation is trivial.

\edgeref{lang_6c130090}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_6c130090}{lang_d24efe0e} in quasipolynomial time. If DNNF compiled to nOBDD in quasipolynomial time, then d-DNNF would compile to nOBDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNNF cannot compile to nOBDD in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_91f812d0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If DNNF compiled to OBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNNF cannot compile to OBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_27fffab2}{lang_89649e36} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_89649e36} in quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_6c130090}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_1afefbe2}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_6c130090}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If DNNF compiled to SDD in quasipolynomial time, then d-DNNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNNF cannot compile to SDD in quasipolynomial time.

Therefore DNNF cannot compile to SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_4e62a038}{lang_6c130090} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_3bebcab7}{lang_6c130090} in quasipolynomial time. If DNNF compiled to uFBDD in quasipolynomial time, then DNNF would compile to d-DNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore DNNF cannot compile to uFBDD in quasipolynomial time.

If DNNF compiled to uOBDD in quasipolynomial time, then d-DNNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Rewrite each decision node labeled $x$ as $(x \wedge D_0) \vee (\neg x \wedge D_1)$, where $D_0$ and $D_1$ are the rewritings of the 0- and 1-successors. The introduced $\vee$-gates are decision gates and the $\wedge$-gates are decomposable, so all $\vee$-gates arise from decision node rewrites, yielding a \langref{dec-DNNF} in linear time \citep{Amarilli_2018}.

Consider the \langref{DNF} formula $\Pi_n= igvee_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_{2i} \wedge x_{2i+1})$. This formula is in prime implicants form and each clause in $\Pi_n$ is an essential prime implicant of it. Hence its negation $
eg\Pi_n$ lies in \langref{IP}.

Since the early work of Quine, we know that the number of essential prime implicates of a formula is a lower bound on the number of clauses that can be found in any \langref{CNF} representation of it. However, since $\Pi_n$ has $2^n$ prime implicants, $
eg\Pi_n$ has $2^n$ prime implicates, so any \langref{CNF} representation of $
eg\Pi_n$ must have at least $2^n$ clauses.

A Shannon tree for any \langref{MODS} formula following order $<$ can be generated in polynomial time. This tree can be reduced into a corresponding \langfam{OBDD}{<} in polynomial time \citep{Darwiche_2002}.

\citet{Kaleyski_2016} constructs a family of Boolean functions $\psi_i$ with \langref{MODS} size $\Theta(2^{2i})$ but \langref{PI} size $\Omega(2^{(i-1)^2/4})$, ruling out a polynomial compilation. Whether an exponential separation exists remains open.

Rewrite each decision node labeled $x$ as $(x \wedge D_0) \vee (\neg x \wedge D_1)$, where $D_0, D_1$ are the rewritings of the 0- and 1-successors. The $\wedge$-gates are decomposable, yielding a \langref{DNNF} in linear time \citep{Amarilli_2018}.

Consider the Boolean functions $\psi_n,\phi_n:\{0,1\}^{n^2}\to\{0,1\}$ that respectively test whether, in an $n \times n$ Boolean matrix, either the number of 1's is odd and there is a full row of 1's, or the number of 1's is even and there is a column full of 1's. An \langref{FBDD} of size $O(n^2)$ may simulate either $\psi_n$ or $\phi_n$, and thus the \langref{uFBDD} $\psi_n\vee\phi_n$ is of size $O(n^2)$; however, an \langref{FBDD} for $\psi_n\vee\phi_n$ is provably of size $2^{\Omega(n^{1/2})}$ \citep{Amarilli_2018}.

Construct a right-linear v-tree from the variable order, then rewrite each decision node labeled $x$ as $(x \wedge D_0) \vee (\neg x \wedge D_1)$, where $D_0, D_1$ are the rewritings of the 0- and 1-successors. The v-tree alignment ensures every $\wedge$-gate respects the variable partition, yielding an \langref{SDNNF} in linear time \citep{Amarilli_2018}.

Fix any variable order $<$ and let $T$ be the corresponding right-linear vtree. Rewriting each decision node labeled $x$ as $(x \wedge D_0) \vee (\neg x \wedge D_1)$ yields a structured output that respects $T$, and all introduced $\vee$-gates are decision gates, producing \langfam{dec-SDNNF}{T} in linear time.

Fix any variable order $<$ and let $T$ be the corresponding right-linear vtree. Every \langfam{OBDD}{<} is a right-linear \langfam{SDD}{T}, and this representation is compressed (canonical), so compilation to \langfam{cSDD}{T} is polynomial-time.
\edgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_43a33aec} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_43a33aec}{lang_e827cf31} in polynomial time. Therefore OBDD$_<$ compiles to nOBDD$_<$ in polynomial time.

For any fixed variable order $<$, every \langfam{OBDD}{<} is already a \langfam{uOBDD}{<} formula respecting the same order $<$. Thus, for each $<$, \langfam{OBDD}{<} $\subset$ \langfam{uOBDD}{<}, and the compilation is the identity.
\edgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_8cf1da0e} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_8cf1da0e}{lang_d7403a53} in polynomial time. Therefore OBDD$_<$ compiles to TDD in polynomial time.

\citet[Theorem 17]{Capelli_2026} show that an \langfam{OBDD}{<} over order $\pi$ can be converted in linear time into an equivalent \langfam{TDD}{T} respecting the corresponding linear vtree $T_{\pi^{-1}}$, with size at most three times the input size.
As defined by \citet{Darwiche_2002}, \langref{PI} is a subset of \langref{CNF}. Therefore, a polynomial compilation is trivial.

The Pairwise \langref{CNF} formula $\Sigma_n = \bigwedge_{i=0}^{n-1}(x_{2i} \vee x_{2i+1})$ has $2^n$ prime implicates; its negation $\neg\Sigma_n$ lies in \langref{PI} and therefore has $2^n$ essential prime implicants, forcing any \langref{DNF} representation to have at least $2^n$ terms \citep{Darwiche_2002}.
\citet[Proposition 11]{Bova_2016} shows that the $JS_n$ has \langref{PI} size $O(n^2)$. \citet[Proposition 12]{Bova_2016} shows that $JS_n$ has \langref{DNNF} size $2^{\Omega(n^2)}$.

The clique detection function $\Sigma_{n,k}$ has $O(n^3)$ prime implicates (hence polynomial \langref{PI} size), but any \langref{FBDD} for $\Sigma_{n,k}$ has exponential size for suitable $k$ \citep{Wegener_1987,Darwiche_2002}.

If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to nOBDD in polynomial time, then d-SDNNF$_T$ would compile to nFBDD in polynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to nOBDD in polynomial time.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
\edgeref{lang_3c803ba1}{lang_e827cf31} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_e827cf31}{lang_d24efe0e} in polynomial time. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ compiles to nOBDD in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_684b1ca7} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_684b1ca7} in quasipolynomial time. If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to OBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time, then d-SDNNF$_T$ would compile to FBDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to OBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_27fffab2}{lang_89649e36} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_89649e36} in quasipolynomial time. If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to PI in quasipolynomial time, then d-SDNNF$_T$ would compile to CNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to PI in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_1afefbe2}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to SDD in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to SDD in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_9c84a267}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to SDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to SDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\langref{SDNNF} is the union of the fixed-vtree families \langfam{SDNNF}{T}. Hence any \langfam{SDNNF}{T} formula is already an \langref{SDNNF} formula, so the compilation is trivial and polynomial-time.

\edgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_4e62a038}{lang_1df07cc3} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_1df07cc3} in polynomial time. If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to uFBDD in polynomial time, then d-SDNNF$_T$ would compile to nFBDD in polynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to uFBDD in polynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_c2df8c2b}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to uOBDD in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to uOBDD in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_684b1ca7} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_684b1ca7} in quasipolynomial time. If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to OBDD in quasipolynomial time, then d-SDNNF$_T$ would compile to FBDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to OBDD in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_981b62f0}{lang_684b1ca7} in quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_684b1ca7} in quasipolynomial time. If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to dec-DNNF in quasipolynomial time, then d-SDNNF$_T$ would compile to FBDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to dec-DNNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_0f27d539}{lang_684b1ca7} in quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_684b1ca7} in quasipolynomial time. If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to dec-SDNNF in quasipolynomial time, then d-SDNNF$_T$ would compile to FBDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to dec-SDNNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_4ae03bc8}{lang_684b1ca7} in quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_684b1ca7} in quasipolynomial time. If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to dec-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then d-SDNNF$_T$ would compile to FBDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to dec-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_82fa749e}{lang_83e3b023} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_83e3b023} in quasipolynomial time. If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to cSDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to cSDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to cSDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to nOBDD$_<$ in polynomial time, then d-SDNNF$_T$ would compile to nFBDD in polynomial time, contradicting the above. quasiDescription.description: The \langref{SDNNF}-to-\langref{nOBDD} simulation can be oriented as in \citet{TCZ_Forthcoming}: for every fixed source vtree $T$, it chooses one output order $<$ depending only on $T$ and gives a quasipolynomial-size \langfam{nOBDD}{<}.

matrix.description:
First, we show no polynomial compilation exists.
\edgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_e827cf31}{lang_1df07cc3} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_1df07cc3} in polynomial time. If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to nOBDD$_<$ in polynomial time, then d-SDNNF$_T$ would compile to nFBDD in polynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to nOBDD$_<$ in polynomial time.

Now, we show a quasipolynomial compilation exists.
The \langref{SDNNF}-to-\langref{nOBDD} simulation can be oriented as in \citet{TCZ_Forthcoming}: for every fixed source vtree $T$, it chooses one output order $<$ depending only on $T$ and gives a quasipolynomial-size \langfam{nOBDD}{<}.

\edgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_43a33aec}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to uOBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to uOBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_d7403a53}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to TDD in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to TDD in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_8cf1da0e}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_4c204bf3}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If SDNNF$_T$ compiled to TDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then DNF would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore SDNNF$_T$ cannot compile to TDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4e62a038} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_89649e36} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to CNF in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to CNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to CNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_4e62a038} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_83e3b023} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to cSDD in quasipolynomial time, then FBDD would compile to cSDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to cSDD in quasipolynomial time.

Rewrite each decision node labeled $x$ as $(x \wedge D_0) \vee (\neg x \wedge D_1)$, where $D_0, D_1$ are the rewritings of the 0- and 1-successors. The $\wedge$-gates are decomposable, and unambiguity of the \langref{uFBDD} ensures all introduced $\vee$-gates are deterministic, yielding a \langref{d-DNNF} in linear time \citep{Amarilli_2018}.

\edgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_4e62a038} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to d-SDNNF in quasipolynomial time, then FBDD would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to d-SDNNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4e62a038} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to d-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4e62a038} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4c204bf3} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to DNF in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to DNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to DNF in quasipolynomial time.
\edgeref{lang_4e62a038}{lang_1df07cc3} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_1df07cc3}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. Therefore uFBDD compiles to DNNF in polynomial time.

Consider the Boolean functions $\phi_n, \psi_n: \{0,1\}^{n^2}\to\{0,1\}$, which respectively test whether, in an $n\times n$ Boolean matrix, that the number of 1's is odd and there is a row full of 1's, and that the number of 1's is even and there is a column full of 1's. \citet{Bova_2016} and \citet{Wegener_2000} show that an \langref{FBDD} expression for $\phi_n \vee \psi_n$ has necessarily size $2^{\Omega(n^{1/2})}$; but since an \langref{FBDD} of size $O(n^2)$ may test $\phi_n$ or $\psi_n$ separately (shown by \citet[Proposition 3.1]{Amarilli_2018}), a \langref{uFBDD} for $\phi_n\vee \psi_n$ may be obtained by simply adding an $\vee$-gate joining these two \langref{FBDD} expressions, since $\phi_n \wedge \psi_n$ is unsatisfiable.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4e62a038} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_6ae90adc}{lang_4c204bf3} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4c204bf3} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to IP in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to DNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to IP in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4e62a038} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_e02902d0}{lang_89649e36} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_89649e36} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to MODS in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to CNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to MODS in quasipolynomial time.
As defined by \citet{Amarilli_2018}, \langref{uFBDD} is the subset of \langref{nFBDD} satisfying unambiguity.

\edgeref{lang_4e62a038}{lang_1df07cc3} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_1df07cc3}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_3bebcab7}{lang_5bf00851} in polynomial time. Therefore uFBDD compiles to NNF in polynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_4e62a038} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_d24efe0e} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to nOBDD in quasipolynomial time, then FBDD would compile to nOBDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to nOBDD in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4e62a038} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_91f812d0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to OBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to OBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4e62a038} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_27fffab2}{lang_89649e36} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_89649e36} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to PI in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to CNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to PI in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_4e62a038} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_1afefbe2}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to SDD in quasipolynomial time, then FBDD would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to SDD in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4e62a038} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_9c84a267}{lang_91f812d0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to SDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to SDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_4e62a038} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_b13b0d78}{lang_d24efe0e} in quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_d24efe0e} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to SDNNF in quasipolynomial time, then FBDD would compile to nOBDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to SDNNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4e62a038} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_3c803ba1} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_4e62a038} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_c2df8c2b}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to uOBDD in quasipolynomial time, then FBDD would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to uOBDD in quasipolynomial time.

If uFBDD compiled to OBDD in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to OBDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to OBDD in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_ea9b5299}{lang_4e62a038} in quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_981b62f0}{lang_684b1ca7} in quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_ea9b5299}{lang_684b1ca7} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to dec-DNNF in quasipolynomial time, then d-SDNNF would compile to FBDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to dec-DNNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4e62a038} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_0f27d539}{lang_b9d72a7c} in quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_b9d72a7c} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to dec-SDNNF in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to OBDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to dec-SDNNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4e62a038} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_4ae03bc8}{lang_91f812d0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to dec-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. If uFBDD compiled to cSDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uFBDD cannot compile to cSDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4e62a038} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_e827cf31}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_3c803ba1} in quasipolynomial time. If uFBDD compiled to nOBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. If uFBDD compiled to uOBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. \edgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_4e62a038} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_d7403a53}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_c2df8c2b}{lang_4e62a038} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_4e62a038}{lang_6c130090} in polynomial time. Therefore uOBDD compiles to d-DNNF in polynomial time.

Construct a right-linear v-tree from the variable order, then rewrite each decision node labeled $x$ as $(x \wedge D_0) \vee (\neg x \wedge D_1)$, where $D_0, D_1$ are the rewritings of the 0- and 1-successors. The v-tree alignment ensures structuredness, and unambiguity of the \langref{uOBDD} makes all introduced $\vee$-gates deterministic, yielding a \langref{d-SDNNF} in linear time \citep{Amarilli_2018}.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_c2df8c2b} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If uOBDD compiled to d-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uOBDD cannot compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_c2df8c2b} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4c204bf3} in quasipolynomial time. If uOBDD compiled to DNF in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to DNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uOBDD cannot compile to DNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_c2df8c2b}{lang_4e62a038} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_4e62a038}{lang_1df07cc3} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_1df07cc3}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. Therefore uOBDD compiles to DNNF in polynomial time.

The weighted sum function $WS_n$ has polynomial-size \langref{uOBDD} formulas: \citet{Wegener_2000} gives polynomial-size \langref{nOBDD}{<} formulas for $WS_n$ under every variable ordering $<$, and the pointer-guess construction has at most one accepting guess, so the formula is unambiguous. By \citet{Savicky_Zak_1996}, every \langref{FBDD} for $WS_n$ has size $2^{n-o(n)}$. Hence no quasipolynomial compilation from \langref{uOBDD} to \langref{FBDD} is possible.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_c2df8c2b} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_6ae90adc}{lang_4c204bf3} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_4c204bf3} in quasipolynomial time. If uOBDD compiled to IP in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to DNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uOBDD cannot compile to IP in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_c2df8c2b} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_e02902d0}{lang_89649e36} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_89649e36} in quasipolynomial time. If uOBDD compiled to MODS in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to CNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uOBDD cannot compile to MODS in quasipolynomial time.
\edgeref{lang_c2df8c2b}{lang_4e62a038} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_4e62a038}{lang_1df07cc3} in polynomial time. Therefore uOBDD compiles to nFBDD in polynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_c2df8c2b}{lang_4e62a038} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_4e62a038}{lang_1df07cc3} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_1df07cc3}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_3bebcab7}{lang_5bf00851} in polynomial time. Therefore uOBDD compiles to NNF in polynomial time.
As defined by \citet{Amarilli_2018}, \langref{uOBDD} is the subset of \langref{nOBDD} satisfying unambiguity.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_c2df8c2b} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_91f812d0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If uOBDD compiled to OBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uOBDD cannot compile to OBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_c2df8c2b} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_27fffab2}{lang_89649e36} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_89649e36} in quasipolynomial time. If uOBDD compiled to PI in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to CNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uOBDD cannot compile to PI in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_c2df8c2b} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_9c84a267}{lang_91f812d0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If uOBDD compiled to SDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uOBDD cannot compile to SDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.
\edgeref{lang_c2df8c2b}{lang_d24efe0e} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_d24efe0e}{lang_b13b0d78} in polynomial time. Therefore uOBDD compiles to SDNNF in polynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_c2df8c2b} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_3c803ba1} in quasipolynomial time. If uOBDD compiled to SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uOBDD cannot compile to SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.
As defined by \citet{Amarilli_2018}, \langref{uOBDD} is the subset of \langref{uFBDD} satisfying an order of the variables, so compilation entails discarding the order.

The hidden weighted bit function $HWB_n$ has polynomial-size \langref{uOBDD} representations under a fixed order $<$: $HWB_n = \bigvee_{k=1}^n(E_k^n(x) \wedge x_k)$, where each branch for $k$ tests the variables in the same order to verify the exact Hamming weight and $x_k=1$, and the branches are disjoint. Thus the representation is unambiguous and has polynomial size, as in the 2-\langref{OBDD}/\langref{FBDD} constructions discussed by \citet{Wegener_2000}. Bryant's hidden-weighted-bit lower bound says every \langref{OBDD} formula for $HWB_n$, regardless of variable order $<$, has exponential size \citep{Bryant_1986,Wegener_2000}. Hence no quasipolynomial compilation to \langref{OBDD} is possible.

\edgeref{lang_ea9b5299}{lang_c2df8c2b} in quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_981b62f0}{lang_684b1ca7} in quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_ea9b5299}{lang_684b1ca7} in quasipolynomial time. If uOBDD compiled to dec-DNNF in quasipolynomial time, then d-SDNNF would compile to FBDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uOBDD cannot compile to dec-DNNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_c2df8c2b} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_0f27d539}{lang_b9d72a7c} in quasipolynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_b9d72a7c} in quasipolynomial time. If uOBDD compiled to dec-SDNNF in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to OBDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uOBDD cannot compile to dec-SDNNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_c2df8c2b} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_4ae03bc8}{lang_91f812d0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If uOBDD compiled to dec-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uOBDD cannot compile to dec-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_c2df8c2b} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_82fa749e}{lang_91f812d0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If uOBDD compiled to cSDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uOBDD cannot compile to cSDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_c2df8c2b} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_e827cf31}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_3c803ba1} in quasipolynomial time. If uOBDD compiled to nOBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uOBDD cannot compile to nOBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_c2df8c2b} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_43a33aec}{lang_91f812d0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If uOBDD compiled to uOBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uOBDD cannot compile to uOBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_c2df8c2b} in at worst quasipolynomial time. \edgeref{lang_8cf1da0e}{lang_91f812d0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_83e3b023}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If uOBDD compiled to TDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then cSDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore uOBDD cannot compile to TDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_b9d72a7c} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_89649e36} in quasipolynomial time. If OBDD compiled to CNF in quasipolynomial time, then OBDD$_<$ would compile to CNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore OBDD cannot compile to CNF in quasipolynomial time.
As established by \citet{Darwiche_2011}, every \langref{OBDD} is a right-linear \langref{SDD}, which is already compressed (canonical). Thus, \langref{cSDD} strictly generalizes \langref{OBDD}.

\edgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_d7403a53} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_d7403a53}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_ea9b5299}{lang_6c130090} in polynomial time. Therefore OBDD compiles to d-DNNF in polynomial time.
\edgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_d7403a53} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_d7403a53}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. Therefore OBDD compiles to d-SDNNF in polynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_3c803ba1} in quasipolynomial time. If OBDD compiled to d-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then OBDD would compile to SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore OBDD cannot compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_b9d72a7c} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_4c204bf3} in quasipolynomial time. If OBDD compiled to DNF in quasipolynomial time, then OBDD$_<$ would compile to DNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore OBDD cannot compile to DNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_d7403a53} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_d7403a53}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_ea9b5299}{lang_b13b0d78} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_b13b0d78}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. Therefore OBDD compiles to DNNF in polynomial time.
As defined by \citet{Darwiche_2002}, \langref{OBDD} is a subset of \langref{FBDD}. Therefore a polynomial time compilation is trivial.

\edgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_b9d72a7c} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_6ae90adc}{lang_4c204bf3} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_4c204bf3} in quasipolynomial time. If OBDD compiled to IP in quasipolynomial time, then OBDD$_<$ would compile to DNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore OBDD cannot compile to IP in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_b9d72a7c} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_e02902d0}{lang_89649e36} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_89649e36} in quasipolynomial time. If OBDD compiled to MODS in quasipolynomial time, then OBDD$_<$ would compile to CNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore OBDD cannot compile to MODS in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_c2df8c2b} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_c2df8c2b}{lang_4e62a038} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_4e62a038}{lang_1df07cc3} in polynomial time. Therefore OBDD compiles to nFBDD in polynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_d7403a53} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_d7403a53}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_ea9b5299}{lang_b13b0d78} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_b13b0d78}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_3bebcab7}{lang_5bf00851} in polynomial time. Therefore OBDD compiles to NNF in polynomial time.
\edgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_c2df8c2b} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_c2df8c2b}{lang_d24efe0e} in polynomial time. Therefore OBDD compiles to nOBDD in polynomial time.

Suppose an order $<$ existed so that \langref{OBDD} compiled to \langfam{OBDD}{<}. Take the first $2n$ variables in that order, call the first half $x_1,\ldots,x_n$ and the second half $y_1,\ldots,y_n$, and consider $EQ_n(x,y)$. This function has linear-size \langref{OBDD}{s} under the interleaved order $x_1,y_1,\ldots,x_n,y_n$, but exponential-size \langfam{OBDD}{<} representations \citep{Wegener_2000}. Thus no order $<$ can exist, and \langref{OBDD} is not quasipolynomial-time compilable to \langfam{OBDD}{<}.

\edgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_b9d72a7c} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_27fffab2}{lang_89649e36} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_d69995dd}{lang_89649e36} in quasipolynomial time. If OBDD compiled to PI in quasipolynomial time, then OBDD$_<$ would compile to CNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore OBDD cannot compile to PI in quasipolynomial time.
\edgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_d7403a53} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_d7403a53}{lang_1afefbe2} in polynomial time. Therefore OBDD compiles to SDD in polynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_9c84a267}{lang_91f812d0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If OBDD compiled to SDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then OBDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore OBDD cannot compile to SDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_d7403a53} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_d7403a53}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_ea9b5299}{lang_b13b0d78} in polynomial time. Therefore OBDD compiles to SDNNF in polynomial time.

For any fixed vtree $T$, a simple function can be chosen so that every \langfam{SDNNF}{T} representation is exponentially large, while some \langref{OBDD} represents it in linear size. Therefore \langref{OBDD} cannot compile to \langfam{SDNNF}{T} in quasipolynomial size \citep{TCZ_Forthcoming}.
\edgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_c2df8c2b} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_c2df8c2b}{lang_4e62a038} in polynomial time. Therefore OBDD compiles to uFBDD in polynomial time.
As defined by \citet{Amarilli_2018}, \langref{OBDD} is the subset of \langref{uOBDD} without any $\vee$-node.
\edgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_0f27d539} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_0f27d539}{lang_981b62f0} in polynomial time. Therefore OBDD compiles to dec-DNNF in polynomial time.

Construct a right-linear v-tree from the variable order, then rewrite each decision node labeled $x$ as $(x \wedge D_0) \vee (\neg x \wedge D_1)$, where $D_0, D_1$ are the rewritings of the 0- and 1-successors. The v-tree alignment ensures structuredness, and since all $\vee$-gates arise from decision node rewrites, every $\vee$-gate is a decision gate, yielding a \langref{dec-SDNNF} in linear time \citep{Amarilli_2018}.

\edgeref{lang_4ae03bc8}{lang_91f812d0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If OBDD compiled to dec-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then OBDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore OBDD cannot compile to dec-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_82fa749e}{lang_91f812d0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If OBDD compiled to cSDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then OBDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore OBDD cannot compile to cSDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_e827cf31}{lang_3c803ba1} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_3c803ba1} in quasipolynomial time. If OBDD compiled to nOBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time, then OBDD would compile to SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore OBDD cannot compile to nOBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_43a33aec}{lang_91f812d0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If OBDD compiled to uOBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time, then OBDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore OBDD cannot compile to uOBDD$_<$ in quasipolynomial time.

\citet[Theorem 17]{Capelli_2026} convert every fixed-order \langfam{OBDD}{<} into an equivalent \langfam{TDD}{T} of linear size for the corresponding linear vtree. Taking the union over orders gives a polynomial compilation from \langref{OBDD} to \langref{TDD}.

\edgeref{lang_8cf1da0e}{lang_91f812d0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_b9d72a7c}{lang_91f812d0} in quasipolynomial time. If OBDD compiled to TDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then OBDD would compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore OBDD cannot compile to TDD$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_981b62f0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_89649e36} in quasipolynomial time. If dec-DNNF compiled to CNF in quasipolynomial time, then FBDD would compile to CNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore dec-DNNF cannot compile to CNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_981b62f0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_83e3b023} in quasipolynomial time. If dec-DNNF compiled to cSDD in quasipolynomial time, then FBDD would compile to cSDD in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore dec-DNNF cannot compile to cSDD in quasipolynomial time.
As defined by \citet{Amarilli_2018}, \langref{dec-DNNF} is the subset of \langref{d-DNNF} satisfying the decision property.

\edgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_981b62f0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If dec-DNNF compiled to d-SDNNF in quasipolynomial time, then FBDD would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore dec-DNNF cannot compile to d-SDNNF in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_981b62f0} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_91f812d0}{lang_ea9b5299} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_ea9b5299} in quasipolynomial time. If dec-DNNF compiled to d-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time, then FBDD would compile to d-SDNNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore dec-DNNF cannot compile to d-SDNNF$_T$ in quasipolynomial time.

\edgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_981b62f0} in polynomial time. \nedgeref{lang_684b1ca7}{lang_4c204bf3} in quasipolynomial time. If dec-DNNF compiled to DNF in quasipolynomial time, then FBDD would compile to DNF in at most quasipolynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore dec-DNNF cannot compile to DNF in quasipolynomial time.
\edgeref{lang_981b62f0}{lang_6c130090} in polynomial time. \edgeref{lang_6c130090}{lang_3bebcab7} in polynomial time. Therefore dec-DNNF compiles to DNNF in polynomial time.

If dec-DNNF compiled to FBDD in polynomial time, then dec-SDNNF$_T$ would compile to nFBDD in polynomial time, contradicting the above. Therefore dec-DNNF cannot compile to FBDD in polynomial time.

quasiDescription.description: \citet[Theorem 2]{Bollig_2018} shows that \langref{SDNNF} can be compiled into \langref{nOBDD} by adapting the compilation of \citet{Beame_2015} from \langref{DNNF} to \langref{nFBDD}; \citet[Proposition 2]{Bollig_2018} shows that this \langref{nOBDD} is unambiguous if the \langref{SDNNF} is deterministic. \citet[Theorem 2]{Bollig_2018} shows that \langref{SDNNF} can be compiled into \langref{nOBDD} by adapting the compilation of \citet{Beame_2015} from \langref{DNNF} to \langref{nFBDD}; \citet[Proposition 2]{Bollig_2018} shows that this \langref{nOBDD} is unambiguous if the \langref{SDNNF} is deterministic. Additionally, the same compilation yields an \langref{OBDD} if the input is a \langref{dec-SDNNF} since there are no $\vee$-gates that are not decision gates in a \langref{dec-SDNNF}.
As defined by \citet{Amarilli_2018}, a \langref{dec-SDNNF} is simply a structured \langref{dec-DNNF}, so discarding the v-tree completes the compilation.

\citet{Bova_2016_a} gives functions $F_n$ with polynomial-size \langfam{cSDD}{T} representations over explicit vtrees. Conditioning the auxiliary variables of $F_n$ recovers $HWB_n$, and conditioning a \langfam{OBDD}{<} preserves the order and cannot increase size. Since \citet{Bryant_1986,Wegener_2000} give exponential \langfam{OBDD}{<} size for $HWB_n$ for every order, every \langfam{OBDD}{<} for $F_n$ is exponential. Hence \langfam{cSDD}{T} is not quasipolynomial-time compilable to \langfam{OBDD}{<}.
